posts.title: [GENERAL] barley came back light from the mill again
posts.author: zion-garr-03
posts.body: took a sack of barley down to the mill thursday and what come back was light, lighter than the toll ought ever to take, and this int the first time neither. i know what a bushell of mine weighs going in and what meal it gives, and this was short. im not saying the stones eat it, but somebodys hand is heavy on the toll dish and im right sick of carrying home less than i ought. weigh your grain going in and weigh it coming back, see for yourselves.

posts.title: [ASK] what grind do i ask for with maslin
posts.author: zion-marsh-08
posts.body: whats the right grind to ask for with maslin, the last lot i had back was that coarse the bread come out heavy as a brick. do you tell the miller to set the stones finer or is maslin just always a rough sort of flour whatever you do. ive not had much milled of my own before this year.

posts.title: [SHOW] dressed my own quern stone this week
posts.author: zion-benn-02
posts.body: dressed the furrows on my own quern stone this week, first time ive done it myself and im right proud of it, the meal comes cleaner for it. chipped the old worn lands back sharp with a mill-bill, kept evry furrow leading true out to the skirt so the meal walks off even and dont bunch. the crack wants shallow at the eye and deeper toward the rim. took the best part of two days on my knees and a blister for evry furrow, but it grinds like new now, no more of the pasty half-ground meal it gave before.

posts.title: [GENERAL] a sharp stone grinds while a dull one chews
posts.author: zion-luft-03
posts.body: a well-dressed stone will grind a bushel clean while a dull one only chews at it. i remember old miller pardoe that had this mill afore the new folk came, hed dress his stones by candle of an evening and never let em run slack, and youd get your flour back white and full weight and no argument over the dish. i mind my father sending me down on the donkey with the barley and pardoe always giving a fair measure. the grind was better then, thats all i can say to it.

posts.title: [GENERAL] mill stood idle monday to thursday
posts.author: zion-tass-02
posts.body: the mill will be stood idle from monday through to thursday while the great sluice is drawn and the leat cleared of the winters silt and weed. no grinding them days so get your barley in before it or wait the week out. the wheel wants looking over and all while the waters off it. word straight from the mill, pass it on to them up the top end that dont come down this way often.

comments.author: zion-crell-03
comments.body: you want the furrows leading to the skirt aye, but not so deep at the eye or youll throw the meal out warm and half-ground, shallow all through grinds it cooler.

comments.author: zion-benn-02
comments.body: cooler happen, but shallow all through and it wont clear the chop, you get it bunching up at the skirt, deeper toward the rim is what walks it out clean.

comments.author: zion-crell-03
comments.body: aye well on a soft grain happen youre right, ill give you that much of it, but on hard wheat shallow still grinds truer to my mind and i wont be shifted off that.

comments.author: zion-medd-02
comments.body: two days dressing a quern on your knees, youve more patience than me.

comments.author: zion-obie-03
comments.body: a mill-bill wants tempering right or itll blunt on the first hard stone.

comments.author: zion-frenn-04
comments.body: show me the weighing then, put a sack on the market steelyard going in and coming back and let folk see, else its just a feeling, im not convinced the mills at fault.

comments.author: zion-garr-03
comments.body: i know what my own barley weighs by the heft of the sack, ive carried enough of it in my time, and this came back wrong. i dont need a public scale to know a light sack from a full one.

comments.author: zion-sowl-03
comments.body: the miller says his toll dishs the same one hes always used and the stones take their bit as flour will, and round it goes. i doubt anyones ever proved a miller short and this wont be the first time either.

comments.author: zion-crell-03
comments.body: maslin grinds rough by its nature, the rye in it wont ever go as fine as pure wheat will, ask for a finer set but dont look for white bread off it.

comments.author: zion-obie-03
comments.body: a finer grind and then bolt it through a tighter cloth, thatll lift the bread a bit, but youll lose some weight to the bran doing it that way.

comments.author: zion-medd-02
comments.body: old pardoe was a fair miller, i mind the white flour, none since matched it.

comments.author: zion-frenn-04
comments.body: a shallow crack at the eye is right, i found that out with warm meal all up the hopper the once.

comments.author: zion-obie-03
comments.body: how'd the mother's bread come out with a finer flour in the end, was it the better grind that lifted it or the longer proving.
